audit_risk_assessments.rationale: NHS operations represent 75% of revenue with high budget cycle dependency and Q4 renewal concentration
Revenue recognition volatility between quarters indicates pipeline management control weaknesses
Processing sensitive NHS patient data under GDPR with potential £20M+ penalties requires strong controls
Single contract represents $4.2M TCV creating significant concentration risk with cross-border regulatory complexity
38% technical debt allocation indicates capacity constraints; successful 7.0 launch demonstrates improved execution
Healthcare data security requires robust controls; current maturity is strong but threat landscape evolving

audit_plans.name: NHS Revenue Recognition Audit Plan
audit_plans.scope_description: All NHS contracts with TCV > £500K, Ireland HSE contract, Q1-Q4 2024 revenue transactions
audit_plans.methodology: Substantive testing of revenue transactions, control testing of deal approval process, analytical review of pipeline conversion

audit_plans.name: GDPR and NHS DSPT Compliance Audit Plan
audit_plans.scope_description: All personal data processing activities, NHS trust data connections, data retention practices
audit_plans.methodology: Control testing, documentation review, technical vulnerability assessment, staff awareness testing

audit_plans.name: Development Capitalization Audit Plan
audit_plans.scope_description: HealthRoster 7.0 development costs, Q1-Q4 2024 capitalized development, technical debt remediation costs
audit_plans.methodology: Capitalization testing per IAS 38, cost allocation review, benefit realization analysis

audit_plans.name: AI Model Governance Audit Plan
objectives: Assess AI model risk framework adequacy
audit_plans.scope_description: Predictive scheduling models, demand forecasting algorithms, model training data, output validation
audit_plans.methodology: Model documentation review, algorithm testing, bias analysis, clinical risk assessment per DCB0129

audit_findings.title: Inadequate Disaster Recovery Testing Frequency
audit_findings.criteria: ISO 22301 requires annual DR testing with documented results; NHS DSPT requires evidence of business continuity capability
audit_findings.condition: DR testing conducted only once in 18 months with incomplete restoration validation for HealthRoster database
audit_findings.cause: Resource constraints and production change freeze periods limited testing windows; no formal DR testing schedule maintained
audit_findings.effect: Potential 48+ hour RTO for HealthRoster in disaster scenario vs 4-hour SLA commitment to NHS trusts
audit_findings.recommendation: Establish quarterly DR testing schedule with automated test orchestration; implement tabletop exercises between full tests
audit_findings.management_response: Accepted. Will implement quarterly testing by Q2 2025 with dedicated DR testing team

audit_findings.title: Insufficient Cloud Access Review Documentation
audit_findings.criteria: ISO 27001 A.9.2.5 requires periodic review of user access rights with documented evidence
audit_findings.condition: Azure AD privileged access reviews completed but retention of review decisions limited to 90 days
audit_findings.cause: Default Azure AD audit log retention settings not aligned with compliance requirements
audit_findings.effect: Unable to demonstrate continuous access governance to NHS DSPT assessors; potential compliance gap
audit_findings.recommendation: Extend Azure AD audit log retention to 2 years; implement automated access review evidence archival
audit_findings.management_response: Implemented extended retention and automated archival in December 2024

audit_findings.title: Revenue Recognition Timing Controls Weakness
audit_findings.criteria: IFRS 15 requires revenue recognition when performance obligations are satisfied; ASC 606 alignment for US GAAP comparability
audit_findings.condition: 15% of Q4 2024 deals showed revenue recognition within 5 days of quarter-end with limited documentation of performance obligation completion
audit_findings.cause: Sales pressure to meet quarterly targets combined with manual deal approval process lacking automated validation
audit_findings.effect: Potential revenue timing misstatement; auditor concern on cut-off procedures; investor confidence risk
audit_findings.recommendation: Implement automated deal stage gates with performance obligation checklist; require controller sign-off for deals > £100K recognized within 10 days of quarter-end
audit_findings.management_response: Partially accept. Will implement enhanced controls for deals > £250K initially

audit_findings.title: Customer Concentration Disclosure Gap
audit_findings.criteria: IFRS 8 requires disclosure of major customers representing > 10% revenue; best practice includes concentration risk disclosure
audit_findings.condition: Ireland HSE contract ($4.2M TCV) represents 8.5% of annual revenue but concentration risk not disclosed in investor materials
audit_findings.cause: Threshold-based disclosure policy focused on 10% limit; no policy for emerging concentration risks
audit_findings.effect: Investors may not have complete picture of customer concentration risk; governance gap if contract lost
audit_findings.recommendation: Implement proactive disclosure for customers > 5% revenue with trend analysis; add concentration risk section to investor materials
audit_findings.management_response: Accepted. Will update investor disclosure policy and Q2 2025 investor materials

audit_findings.title: Pipeline Qualification Criteria Inconsistency
audit_findings.criteria: Internal pipeline management policy requires consistent stage definitions and documented qualification criteria
audit_findings.condition: Analysis of Q1-Q4 2024 pipeline shows 34% of deals moved backward in stages; $2.8M pipeline pushed between quarters without documented justification
audit_findings.cause: Sales team applying inconsistent qualification criteria; CRM system allows stage changes without mandatory reason codes
audit_findings.effect: Revenue forecasting unreliability (Q1 at 84% vs Q4 at 106%); board visibility on true pipeline health compromised
audit_findings.recommendation: Implement mandatory qualification criteria checklist at each stage gate; require manager approval for backward stage movements; weekly pipeline quality review
audit_findings.management_response: Accepted. CRM enhancement in progress; new qualification framework launching March 2025

risks.name: NHS Budget Cycle Revenue Dependency
risks.description: Company's heavy dependence on NHS annual budget cycles creates quarterly revenue unpredictability, with procurement delays causing pipeline pushes between quarters

risks.name: Customer Concentration - Ireland HSE
risks.description: Ireland HSE contract at $4.2M TCV represents significant single customer concentration risk with cross-border regulatory complexity

risks.name: GDPR Non-Compliance Penalty Exposure
risks.description: Processing NHS patient data under GDPR with potential £20M+ penalties for material violations affecting data subject rights

risks.name: Technical Debt Capacity Constraint
risks.description: 38% engineering resource allocation to technical debt limits new feature development capacity and competitive response agility

risks.name: Competitive Threat - Rotageek Series C
risks.description: Well-funded competitor Rotageek ($45M Series C) creating pricing pressure and accelerating feature development competition

risks.name: Q4 Renewal Cohort Concentration
risks.description: 28% of annual ARR concentrated in Q4 renewal cohort creating significant churn exposure in single quarter

risks.name: Revenue Recognition Timing Misstatement
risks.description: Swing from 84% to 106% quarterly attainment indicates potential revenue timing manipulation or inadequate deal qualification controls

risks.name: NHS Digital DSPT Non-Compliance
risks.description: Failure to maintain NHS Digital Data Security and Protection Toolkit compliance could result in loss of NHS contracts

risks.name: AI Model Bias and Clinical Safety
risks.description: Predictive scheduling AI models may exhibit bias affecting staff allocation or patient safety outcomes

risks.name: Medical Device Regulation Applicability
risks.description: Expanding AI capabilities in clinical workflow integration may trigger EU Medical Device Regulation compliance requirements

risks.name: Post-Brexit Data Transfer Restrictions
risks.description: UK-EU data adequacy decisions and NHS data sovereignty requirements creating cross-border processing complexity

risks.name: Cloud Service Provider Dependency
risks.description: Critical dependency on Microsoft Azure for all production systems with limited multi-cloud capability

risks.name: Cybersecurity Breach - Healthcare Data
risks.description: Data breach affecting NHS patient or staff data could result in regulatory penalties, reputational damage, and contract termination

risks.name: Key Person Dependency
risks.description: Critical dependencies on key technical and domain experts in healthcare workforce management

risks.name: EBITDA Margin Target Miss
risks.description: EBITDA margins at 11% vs 14% target while headcount grows from 412 to 456 FTEs indicates potential margin compression

risks.name: Third-Party Integration Failure
risks.description: Failure of critical NHS system integrations (ESR, spine) could impact service delivery

risks.name: Public Sector Contract Renewal Failure
risks.description: Failure to successfully re-compete existing NHS framework agreements could result in significant revenue loss

risks.name: Product Liability - Clinical Decisions
risks.description: Workforce scheduling recommendations affecting clinical staffing levels may create professional liability exposure

risks.name: Pricing Pressure Margin Erosion
risks.description: Competitive pressure forcing pricing concessions (12 situations in Q1) eroding unit economics

risks.name: Software Development Cost Capitalization
risks.description: Improper capitalization of software development costs affecting financial statement accuracy

risk_assessments.likelihood_rationale: NHS procurement delays continue but diversification efforts reducing dependency
risk_assessments.impact_rationale: 75% revenue exposure remains significant; any NHS budget freeze would materially impact growth

risk_assessments.likelihood_rationale: Strong GDPR controls maintained; no incidents reported; regular DPO oversight
risk_assessments.impact_rationale: Penalty exposure remains £20M+ based on data volume; reputational impact would be severe

risk_assessments.likelihood_rationale: Win rate improved to 34% but Rotageek Tempus acquisition signals continued competitive pressure
risk_assessments.impact_rationale: AI differentiation provides buffer; pricing concessions manageable at current level

risk_assessments.likelihood_rationale: Audit findings identified control gaps; remediation in progress; enhanced monitoring implemented
risk_assessments.impact_rationale: Material misstatement could affect investor confidence and financing terms

risk_assessments.likelihood_rationale: Robust AI governance framework implemented; bias testing automated; clinical safety reviews conducted
risk_assessments.impact_rationale: Patient safety incident could result in regulatory action and NHS contract termination

risk_assessments.likelihood_rationale: Threat landscape increasingly sophisticated; controls strong but require continuous enhancement
risk_assessments.impact_rationale: Healthcare data breach would trigger GDPR penalties, NHS contract review, and significant reputational damage

control_assessments.test_evidence: Deal analysis spreadsheet, controller approval records
control_assessments.deviations_noted: 15% of deals lacked complete performance obligation documentation; 3 deals missing controller approval

control_assessments.test_performed: Evaluated deal stage gate validation; analyzed CRM stage movement data for Q3-Q4 2024
control_assessments.test_evidence: CRM audit report, stage movement analysis
control_assessments.deviations_noted: Control not yet fully implemented; 34% of deals moved backward without documented justification

control_assessments.test_evidence: DR test schedule, test reports, RTO measurements
control_assessments.deviations_noted: Only one DR test in 18 months; HealthRoster database restoration incomplete

control_assessments.test_performed: Reviewed AI bias testing procedures; examined test results for predictive scheduling model
control_assessments.test_evidence: Bias test reports, model cards, fairness metrics dashboard
control_assessments.deviations_noted: None - robust testing framework in place

control_assessments.test_performed: Verified EDR coverage across all endpoints; reviewed SOC monitoring procedures
control_assessments.test_evidence: EDR coverage report, SOC runbooks, incident response times
control_assessments.deviations_noted: Minor - 3 developer machines pending deployment (within 48hr SLA)

control_assessments.test_performed: Validated customer health monitoring for major customers; tested escalation procedures
control_assessments.test_evidence: Health score dashboard, escalation records, executive alerts

control_assessments.test_performed: Reviewed NHS DSPT submission completeness; validated evidence for all assertions
control_assessments.test_evidence: DSPT submission, evidence pack, Standards Met confirmation
control_assessments.deviations_noted: None - all 10 standards met with compliant evidence

control_deficiencies.title: Incomplete Quarter-End Revenue Recognition Documentation
control_deficiencies.description: 15% of deals recognized within 10 days of quarter-end lacked complete performance obligation documentation; 3 deals missing required controller approval
control_deficiencies.root_cause: Manual process with insufficient enforcement; sales pressure to close quarter
control_deficiencies.remediation_plan: Implement automated PO checklist in CPQ; require deal desk sign-off for quarter-end deals

control_deficiencies.title: Deal Stage Gate Validation Not Enforced
control_deficiencies.description: CRM allows stage progression without mandatory checklist completion; 34% of deals moved backward without documented justification
control_deficiencies.root_cause: CRM configuration not enforcing required fields; sales culture prioritizing deal velocity
control_deficiencies.remediation_plan: CRM enhancement to require mandatory fields; implement manager approval for backward movement

control_deficiencies.title: Inadequate DR Testing Frequency
control_deficiencies.description: DR testing conducted only once in 18 months; HealthRoster database restoration validation incomplete
control_deficiencies.root_cause: Resource constraints; production change freeze periods limited windows; no formal testing schedule
control_deficiencies.remediation_plan: Establish quarterly DR testing schedule; implement automated test orchestration; dedicated DR team

control_deficiencies.title: Customer Concentration Disclosure Gap
control_deficiencies.description: Concentration risk for customers approaching 10% threshold not proactively disclosed; Ireland HSE at 8.5% not flagged
control_deficiencies.root_cause: Disclosure policy focused on 10% threshold only; no emerging risk disclosure process
control_deficiencies.remediation_plan: Update disclosure policy for 5%+ customers; add concentration risk section to investor materials

standards_regulations.name: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
standards_regulations.description: EU regulation on data protection and privacy for individuals within the European Union and EEA

standards_regulations.name: NHS Data Security and Protection Toolkit (DSPT)
standards_regulations.description: NHS Digital's data security standard for organizations processing NHS data

standards_regulations.name: ISO 27001:2022
standards_regulations.description: International standard for information security management systems

standards_regulations.name: Clinical Risk Management (DCB0129)
standards_regulations.description: NHS standard for clinical risk management in health IT systems

standards_regulations.name: EU Medical Device Regulation (MDR)
standards_regulations.description: EU regulation for medical devices including software as medical device

standards_regulations.name: IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition
standards_regulations.description: International Financial Reporting Standard for revenue from contracts with customers

standards_regulations.name: Cyber Essentials Plus
standards_regulations.description: UK government-backed cyber security certification scheme

requirements.name: Article 30 - Records of Processing Activities
requirements.description: Maintain records of all processing activities under controller responsibility
requirements.requirement_text: Each controller shall maintain a record of processing activities under its responsibility containing prescribed information

requirements.name: Article 33 - Breach Notification
requirements.description: Notify supervisory authority of personal data breaches within 72 hours
requirements.requirement_text: In the case of a personal data breach, the controller shall without undue delay notify the supervisory authority within 72 hours

requirements.name: DSPT Standard 1 - Leadership
requirements.description: Senior management accountability for data security
requirements.requirement_text: The organisation's leadership has set out its approach to data security and made appropriate resources available

requirements.name: ISO 27001 A.9.2.5 - Access Rights Review
requirements.description: Periodic review of user access rights
requirements.requirement_text: Asset owners shall review users' access rights at regular intervals

requirements.name: DCB0129 Clinical Safety Case
requirements.description: Maintenance of clinical safety case for health IT systems
requirements.requirement_text: The manufacturer shall produce a clinical safety case demonstrating how clinical risks are managed

requirements.name: IFRS 15 Step 5 - Revenue Recognition
requirements.description: Recognize revenue when performance obligations are satisfied
requirements.requirement_text: Revenue shall be recognized when (or as) the entity satisfies a performance obligation

requirements.name: Article 35 - Data Protection Impact Assessment
requirements.description: DPIA required for high-risk processing activities
requirements.requirement_text: Where a type of processing is likely to result in a high risk, the controller shall carry out an assessment of the impact

requirements.name: Article 46 - Appropriate Safeguards for Transfers
requirements.description: Implement appropriate safeguards for international data transfers
requirements.requirement_text: A transfer of personal data to a third country may take place where appropriate safeguards are in place

assessment_methods.name: Substantive Transaction Testing
assessment_methods.description: Detailed testing of individual transactions against supporting documentation and criteria
assessment_methods.sample_size_guidance: 25-50 transactions based on population size and risk level

assessment_methods.name: Control Effectiveness Testing
assessment_methods.description: Testing of control operation and effectiveness through observation, inquiry, and reperformance
assessment_methods.sample_size_guidance: Minimum 25 instances for ongoing controls; 1 instance for periodic controls

assessment_methods.name: Automated Control Testing
assessment_methods.description: Testing of system-enforced controls through configuration review and data analysis
assessment_methods.sample_size_guidance: 100% population for automated extraction; configuration review per system

assessment_methods.name: Compliance Walkthrough
assessment_methods.description: End-to-end walkthrough of processes to validate control design and compliance alignment

assessment_methods.name: Data Analytics Testing
assessment_methods.description: Use of data analytics to test entire populations and identify anomalies

assessment_methods.name: Technical Vulnerability Assessment
assessment_methods.description: Technical testing of security controls through scanning and penetration testing
